title: Define SecAgent RiskOps product scope and architecture
body:
Create initial project documents that define the product direction, system boundaries, threat model, and long-term architecture.

Tasks:
- [ ] Create PROJECT_CHARTER.md
- [ ] Create SYSTEM_ARCHITECTURE.md
- [ ] Create THREAT_MODEL.md
- [ ] Define authorized-use boundary
- [ ] Define autonomy levels
- [ ] Define core modules: SOC, GRC, Remediation, Knowledge

title: Create backend project structure
body:
Set up the initial backend structure for the RiskOps platform.

Tasks:
- [ ] Create backend/app/core
- [ ] Create backend/app/schemas
- [ ] Create backend/app/agents
- [ ] Create backend/app/tools
- [ ] Create backend/app/api
- [ ] Create backend/app/storage
- [ ] Add basic FastAPI health endpoint

title: Implement core data schemas
body:
Implement the first version of the core RiskOps data models.

Tasks:
- [ ] Asset schema
- [ ] Alert schema
- [ ] Finding schema
- [ ] Evidence schema
- [ ] Incident schema
- [ ] Control schema
- [ ] Risk schema
- [ ] ActionPlan schema
- [ ] KnowledgeItem schema
- [ ] AgentRun schema
- [ ] ToolCall schema

title: Set up PostgreSQL schema and persistence layer
body:
Create the initial database structure for storing assets, alerts, findings, evidence, incidents, controls, risks, and audit records.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add database configuration
- [ ] Add migrations
- [ ] Create tables for core schemas
- [ ] Add repository/service layer
- [ ] Add seed script for demo data

title: Implement audit log and evidence trace model
body:
Every agent decision, tool call, finding, and remediation action should be traceable to evidence.

Tasks:
- [ ] Create audit_log table
- [ ] Create evidence hash field
- [ ] Link findings to evidence
- [ ] Link incidents to alerts/findings/evidence
- [ ] Link agent runs to tool calls

title: Implement alert normalization pipeline
body:
Build a normalization layer that converts scanner findings, Suricata sample alerts, Zeek logs, and Linux auth logs into a unified Alert schema.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add parser interface
- [ ] Add SecAgent scanner parser
- [ ] Add Suricata eve.json sample parser
- [ ] Add Zeek conn.log / dns.log sample parser
- [ ] Add Linux auth.log parser
- [ ] Store normalized alerts

title: Implement alert deduplication and grouping
body:
Group repeated alerts by asset, source, rule, entity, and time window to reduce alert noise.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define grouping keys
- [ ] Implement time-window grouping
- [ ] Add duplicate count
- [ ] Preserve original alert references
- [ ] Store AlertGroup records

title: Implement basic risk scoring engine
body:
Calculate risk score using severity, asset criticality, confidence, exposure, correlation count, and known-benign context.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define scoring formula
- [ ] Add asset criticality weight
- [ ] Add correlation bonus
- [ ] Add duplicate penalty
- [ ] Add known-benign reduction
- [ ] Return P0/P1/P2/P3 priority

title: Create AI triage agent for alert groups
body:
The AI triage agent should summarize grouped alerts, explain why they matter, recommend a disposition, and identify whether human review is needed.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define structured AI output schema
- [ ] Implement prompt template
- [ ] Require evidence-grounded summary
- [ ] Output priority, confidence, disposition, and explanation
- [ ] Add fallback behavior for low-confidence analysis

title: Implement skeptic agent for triage validation
body:
Add a second agent that challenges the triage result and checks whether evidence is sufficient, whether the priority is over/understated, and whether suppression is safe.

Tasks:
- [ ] Review AI triage output
- [ ] Check evidence sufficiency
- [ ] Flag overclaiming
- [ ] Flag unsafe suppression
- [ ] Produce validation result

title: Build SOC Inbox frontend
body:
Create the first SOC Inbox UI that shows only actionable incident candidates and summarizes auto-triaged/suppressed alerts.

Tasks:
- [ ] Needs Review view
- [ ] Escalated view
- [ ] Auto-Triaged view
- [ ] Suppressed view
- [ ] Alert group detail page
- [ ] AI triage explanation panel

title: Generate daily SOC briefing
body:
Generate a daily summary of critical incidents, items needing review, auto-triaged alerts, suppressed duplicates, and tuning suggestions.

Tasks:
- [ ] Summarize P0/P1/P2/P3 counts
- [ ] Summarize top noisy rules
- [ ] Summarize top risky assets
- [ ] Include recommended actions
- [ ] Export as Markdown

title: Create initial GRC control library
body:
Create a small built-in control library for NIST CSF 2.0, SOC 2, and CIS Controls mappings.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add control schema
- [ ] Add seed controls
- [ ] Add evidence requirements
- [ ] Add related detection examples
- [ ] Add control status field

title: Implement finding-to-control mapping agent
body:
Map confirmed findings and incidents to GRC controls and generate a control gap explanation.

Tasks:
- [ ] Input incident/finding/evidence
- [ ] Output related controls
- [ ] Output gap explanation
- [ ] Output evidence summary
- [ ] Output confidence score

title: Implement risk register generation
body:
Create risk register entries from confirmed incidents and control gaps.

Tasks:
- [ ] Risk statement
- [ ] Likelihood
- [ ] Impact
- [ ] Owner
- [ ] Status
- [ ] Due date
- [ ] Residual risk

title: Define ActionPlan schema and remediation workflow
body:
Create the structure for controlled remediation plans with preflight, backup, approval, execution, verification, and rollback.

Tasks:
- [ ] ActionPlan schema
- [ ] Precondition schema
- [ ] Backup schema
- [ ] Verification schema
- [ ] Rollback schema
- [ ] Risk level classification

title: Implement policy engine for tool and action approval
body:
Build a policy engine that controls which tools and actions are allowed, blocked, or require human approval.

Tasks:
- [ ] Tool allowlist
- [ ] Target allowlist
- [ ] Risk-based approval
- [ ] Dangerous action blocklist
- [ ] Autonomy level support
- [ ] Approval decision object

title: Implement GitHub remediation adapter
body:
Support safe code remediation by creating branches, applying patches, running checks, and opening pull requests.

Tasks:
- [ ] Create branch
- [ ] Apply patch
- [ ] Commit changes
- [ ] Run test command
- [ ] Create PR
- [ ] Add PR summary

title: Implement Linux lab SSH adapter
body:
Support controlled remediation in a lab Linux server using typed actions instead of arbitrary shell commands.

Tasks:
- [ ] Read file
- [ ] Create backup
- [ ] Patch config key
- [ ] Test nginx config
- [ ] Reload nginx after approval
- [ ] Verify HTTP security headers
- [ ] Rollback backup

title: Build remediation approval queue UI
body:
Create a UI for reviewing proposed remediation actions before execution.

Tasks:
- [ ] Show action summary
- [ ] Show risk level
- [ ] Show diff
- [ ] Show preflight results
- [ ] Show rollback plan
- [ ] Approve / Reject / Edit

title: Design flow-based execution model
body:
Design the internal Flow / Task / Step / ToolCall / Artifact model used by SOC investigations, GRC mapping, remediation workflows, and knowledge review.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define Flow schema
- [ ] Define Task schema
- [ ] Define Step schema
- [ ] Define ToolCall schema
- [ ] Define Artifact schema
- [ ] Link Flow to Evidence
- [ ] Define state transitions

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Flow model supports SOC, GRC, remediation, and knowledge review workflows
- [ ] Each workflow step can be linked to evidence and audit logs
- [ ] State transitions are documented

title: Implement flow runtime state machine
body:
Implement basic state transitions for long-running agent workflows.

States:
- created
- queued
- running
- waiting_for_approval
- completed
- failed
- cancelled

Tasks:
- [ ] Add Flow state enum
- [ ] Add Task state enum
- [ ] Add Step state enum
- [ ] Implement transition validation
- [ ] Log all state transitions to audit trail
- [ ] Add tests for invalid transitions

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Flow, Task, and Step state transitions are enforced
- [ ] Invalid transitions are blocked
- [ ] Every transition is auditable

title: Implement agent activity timeline
body:
Create a UI timeline that shows what each agent did, which tool it called, what evidence it used, and what decision it made.

Tasks:
- [ ] Show agent run timeline
- [ ] Show tool calls
- [ ] Show evidence references
- [ ] Show blocked actions
- [ ] Show decision records
- [ ] Show errors and low-confidence decisions

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A user can inspect why an alert was escalated, auto-triaged, or suppressed
- [ ] Tool calls and evidence references are visible
- [ ] Blocked or approval-required actions are visible

title: Design memory model for SOC, GRC, and remediation
body:
Create memory types for long-term knowledge, working memory, and episodic memory.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define long-term memory schema
- [ ] Define working memory schema
- [ ] Define episodic memory schema
- [ ] Add provenance fields
- [ ] Add TTL fields
- [ ] Add versioning fields
- [ ] Define promotion workflow from candidate to active

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Memory entries include source, confidence, timestamp, TTL, and version
- [ ] AI can only create candidate memory
- [ ] Active memory requires validation or approval

title: Implement supervisor agent and stuck-state detection
body:
Detect repeated tool calls, no-progress loops, unsafe suppression recommendations, and failed remediation retries.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add repeated tool call detection
- [ ] Add max tool call threshold
- [ ] Add no-progress detection
- [ ] Trigger review for unsafe suppression recommendations
- [ ] Add remediation retry limit
- [ ] Store supervisor decision record

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Repetitive or low-progress agent loops are detected
- [ ] Unsafe suppression recommendations trigger skeptic/supervisor review
- [ ] Failed remediation retries stop safely

title: Add evaluation and replay framework design
body:
Design the replay and evaluation framework used to measure triage reliability, suppression safety, GRC mapping quality, and remediation outcomes.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define replay dataset format
- [ ] Define expected labels format
- [ ] Define triage accuracy metrics
- [ ] Define false suppression metrics
- [ ] Define missed escalation metrics
- [ ] Define control mapping metrics
- [ ] Define regression test workflow

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Evaluation design includes false suppression and missed escalation metrics
- [ ] Replay framework can be used before enabling auto-suppression
- [ ] Documentation is added under docs/evaluation-replay.md

title: Add current capability boundaries to README
body:
Document what SecAgent RiskOps currently does and does not do.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add authorized-use boundary
- [ ] Add not-a-SIEM-replacement-yet statement
- [ ] Add no unrestricted autonomous shell statement
- [ ] Add no unapproved medium/high-risk remediation statement
- [ ] Add suppression safety boundary
- [ ] Cross-link SECURITY.md and docs/capability-boundaries.md

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] README contains a Current Capability Boundaries section
- [ ] Boundaries are consistent with SECURITY.md
- [ ] Autonomy levels are referenced

title: Design external intelligence ingestion layer
body:
Design the External Intelligence Ingestion Layer that collects, normalizes, validates, and promotes external security knowledge.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define source registry model
- [ ] Define raw intelligence document schema
- [ ] Define extracted entity schema
- [ ] Define knowledge candidate schema
- [ ] Define promotion workflow
- [ ] Define TTL / provenance / confidence model
- [ ] Define where this layer fits into SOC, GRC, remediation, and knowledge workflows

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Architecture documented in docs/external-intelligence-ingestion.md
- [ ] Source-to-candidate workflow is defined
- [ ] External knowledge cannot directly become active knowledge without validation

title: Implement source registry for approved intelligence sources
body:
Create a registry of approved external intelligence sources.

Tasks:
- [ ] Create SourceRegistryEntry schema
- [ ] Add source_type field
- [ ] Add trust_level field
- [ ] Add update_frequency field
- [ ] Add rate_limit_policy field
- [ ] Add robots_policy / terms field
- [ ] Add enabled/disabled flag
- [ ] Add example source registry JSON

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Connectors can only use registered sources
- [ ] Disabled sources are not fetched
- [ ] Source trust metadata is available to validation and promotion logic

title: Implement raw intelligence document and extracted entity schemas
body:
Implement schemas for storing raw external intelligence and extracted entities.

Tasks:
- [ ] Create RawIntelligenceDocument schema
- [ ] Create ExtractedIntelligenceEntity schema
- [ ] Add content hash field
- [ ] Add source reference field
- [ ] Add retrieval timestamp
- [ ] Add parser version
- [ ] Add processing status
- [ ] Add error metadata

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Raw intelligence and extracted entities are stored separately
- [ ] Every extracted entity links back to raw source evidence
- [ ] Content hash supports deduplication

title: Implement NVD and CISA KEV connectors
body:
Fetch CVE metadata and known exploited vulnerability data from structured official sources.

Tasks:
- [ ] Implement NVD connector skeleton
- [ ] Implement CISA KEV connector skeleton
- [ ] Store raw response as RawIntelligenceDocument
- [ ] Extract CVE entities
- [ ] Deduplicate by CVE ID and content hash
- [ ] Add source provenance
- [ ] Add connector error handling

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CVE entities can be imported from NVD
- [ ] KEV status can be imported from CISA
- [ ] Raw data is preserved with provenance
- [ ] No active knowledge is created directly by connectors

title: Implement EPSS enrichment connector
body:
Enrich CVE entities with EPSS probability and percentile.

Tasks:
- [ ] Implement EPSS connector skeleton
- [ ] Query EPSS by CVE ID
- [ ] Store EPSS probability
- [ ] Store EPSS percentile
- [ ] Store last_updated timestamp
- [ ] Link EPSS enrichment to CVE entity
- [ ] Expose EPSS as risk scoring input

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] CVE entities can include EPSS probability and percentile
- [ ] EPSS data is timestamped
- [ ] Risk scoring can consume EPSS enrichment

title: Implement MITRE ATT&CK enrichment importer
body:
Import ATT&CK tactics and techniques for alert, incident, and control mapping.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define AttackTechnique schema
- [ ] Define AttackTactic schema
- [ ] Import or stub ATT&CK STIX/TAXII data source
- [ ] Store technique IDs and names
- [ ] Link alert/finding types to techniques
- [ ] Expose technique context to triage and GRC mapping

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] ATT&CK technique data can be represented internally
- [ ] Alert/finding types can reference techniques
- [ ] GRC mapping can use ATT&CK context

title: Design crawler safety and governance policy
body:
Define rules for safe crawler and external intelligence behavior.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define allowlist policy
- [ ] Define robots.txt / terms handling
- [ ] Define rate limit policy
- [ ] Define prohibited behaviors
- [ ] Define source reputation model
- [ ] Define conflict handling
- [ ] Define promotion workflow
- [ ] Document no automatic exploit execution

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Crawler governance documented in docs/crawler-safety-governance.md
- [ ] Broad crawling is blocked by default
- [ ] External content is candidate-only until validated

title: Implement knowledge candidate review queue
body:
Prevent raw crawler or connector output from directly becoming active knowledge.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define KnowledgeCandidate schema
- [ ] Add candidate status model
- [ ] Add confidence score
- [ ] Add source reputation score
- [ ] Add reviewer decision fields
- [ ] Add promote / reject / expire transitions
- [ ] Add audit log integration

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Imported intelligence enters candidate state first
- [ ] Candidate promotion is explicit and auditable
- [ ] Expired or rejected candidates are not used in triage or remediation

title: Integrate external intelligence into risk scoring design
body:
Define how external intelligence influences SOC risk scoring.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define risk scoring inputs
- [ ] Define external intelligence weighting
- [ ] Define freshness and TTL handling
- [ ] Define missing-data behavior
- [ ] Define conflict handling

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Risk scoring design can consume CVE/KEV/EPSS/ATT&CK context
- [ ] Missing or stale intelligence does not create false confidence
- [ ] External intelligence source references are preserved in scoring evidence

title: Design authorized security validation layer

Design the layer that performs safe, scoped, authorized checks against target systems.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define AssessmentTarget schema
- [ ] Define AssessmentScope schema
- [ ] Define ValidationJob schema
- [ ] Define ValidationCheck schema
- [ ] Define ScanResult schema
- [ ] Define safety policy integration
- [ ] Define evidence linkage
- [ ] Define how validation results become Findings

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Architecture documented in docs/authorized-security-validation.md
- [ ] Validation workflow documented
- [ ] No-exploit default policy is included
- [ ] Validation output feeds SOC, GRC, remediation, and knowledge workflows

title: Implement assessment scope and authorization model
body:
Ensure validation jobs only run against explicitly authorized targets.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add AssessmentTarget schema
- [ ] Add AssessmentScope schema
- [ ] Add allowed targets field
- [ ] Add allowed ports and protocols
- [ ] Add validity window
- [ ] Add safety level
- [ ] Add rate limit field
- [ ] Add authorization record

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Validation jobs cannot run without an active scope
- [ ] Targets outside scope are blocked
- [ ] Expired scopes are rejected
- [ ] Authorization decisions are auditable

title: Implement scanner orchestrator skeleton
body:
Create a service that schedules and tracks validation checks.

Tasks:
- [ ] Create ValidationJob object
- [ ] Create ValidationCheck queue model
- [ ] Integrate with Flow runtime
- [ ] Integrate with ToolCall model
- [ ] Add status tracking
- [ ] Add error handling
- [ ] Add audit logging

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A validation job can be created from a scope
- [ ] Checks can be queued and status-tracked
- [ ] ToolCall and Evidence linkage is supported

title: Implement safe service discovery checks
body:
Perform read-only service discovery on authorized targets.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add host reachability check
- [ ] Add allowed port discovery
- [ ] Add service fingerprinting placeholder
- [ ] Add banner capture placeholder
- [ ] Store raw output as evidence
- [ ] Enforce rate limiting
- [ ] Block targets outside scope

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Only authorized targets are checked
- [ ] Discovery is read-only
- [ ] Results are stored as evidence
- [ ] Findings can be generated from discovery results

title: Implement web security baseline checks
body:
Check common web security posture without exploitation.

Tasks:
- [ ] Check security headers
- [ ] Check TLS configuration
- [ ] Check HTTP methods
- [ ] Check redirect behavior
- [ ] Check CORS configuration
- [ ] Detect directory listing signal
- [ ] Store evidence for each check

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Web baseline checks are non-destructive
- [ ] Missing or risky configurations generate findings
- [ ] Evidence links are created for every finding

title: Integrate validation findings with external intelligence
body:
Enrich discovered services and versions with NVD, CISA KEV, EPSS, GHSA, OSV, and vendor advisory data.

Tasks:
- [ ] Map service/version to CVE candidates
- [ ] Enrich CVE candidates with CISA KEV status
- [ ] Enrich CVE candidates with EPSS score
- [ ] Link enrichment to evidence
- [ ] Feed enrichment into risk scoring
- [ ] Preserve source references

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Validation findings can use external intelligence context
- [ ] Risk scoring can consume enrichment
- [ ] Missing or low-confidence enrichment does not create false certainty

title: Convert validation results into normalized findings
body:
Convert scanner and validation results into normalized Finding objects.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define ValidationFinding mapping
- [ ] Add finding severity
- [ ] Add finding confidence
- [ ] Add evidence linkage
- [ ] Add asset linkage
- [ ] Add control mapping candidate
- [ ] Add remediation candidate

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Validation output becomes normalized Finding objects
- [ ] Findings link to target, asset, evidence, and validation job
- [ ] Findings can be consumed by SOC, GRC, and remediation workflows

title: Define and enforce no-exploit default policy
body:
Document and enforce that validation is non-destructive and no-exploit by default.

Tasks:
- [ ] Document no exploit execution by default
- [ ] Document no brute force
- [ ] Document no credential attacks
- [ ] Document no payload upload
- [ ] Document no lateral movement
- [ ] Document no destructive checks
- [ ] Define future exception model
- [ ] Add blocked action audit behavior

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Policy documented in docs/validation-safety-policy.md
- [ ] Unsafe actions are blocked by default
- [ ] Future active validation requires explicit policy and approval

title: Implement assessment authorization attestation and evidence references
milestone: v0.1.9 Assessment Authorization and Rules of Engagement
body:
Build the accountable authorization record required before active validation. This extends the baseline assessment model in #38.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add AssessmentAuthorization schema and persistence lifecycle
- [ ] Capture requestor, authorizing party, purpose, validity, and evidence reference
- [ ] Support draft, verified, expired, revoked, and rejected states
- [ ] Apply tenant authorization and separation-of-duties policy where configured
- [ ] Keep raw authorization documents out of general audit logs

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Level 1 or higher validation is denied without verified, current authorization
- [ ] Revocation and expiry prevent new target-facing dispatches
- [ ] Audit events reference protected evidence without copying sensitive contents
- [ ] Unit tests cover missing, expired, revoked, and cross-tenant authorization

title: Implement scope draft and AI interpretation contract
milestone: v0.1.9 Assessment Authorization and Rules of Engagement
body:
Convert optional natural-language scope and Rules of Engagement input into a non-authoritative structured draft.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add AssessmentScopeDraft input and output schemas
- [ ] Extract typed targets, exclusions, ports, protocols, windows, limits, techniques, and stop conditions
- [ ] Return per-field confidence, source rationale, conflicts, and unresolved questions
- [ ] Prevent the model from inferring ownership, adding targets, or weakening deny rules
- [ ] Preserve structured user input separately from model interpretation

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Blank input never produces an unrestricted allow policy
- [ ] Ambiguity that could broaden scope sets needs_clarification
- [ ] Model output cannot activate a scope or authorize a tool call
- [ ] Adversarial and contradictory input fixtures are covered by tests

title: Build assessment scope and Rules of Engagement review UI
milestone: v0.1.9 Assessment Authorization and Rules of Engagement
body:
Build the preflight experience for authorization, structured scope, optional natural-language instructions, and effective-policy confirmation.

Tasks:
- [ ] Add authorization attestation and evidence-reference step
- [ ] Add structured allow targets, exclusions, ports, protocols, time window, and safety controls
- [ ] Add optional natural-language input and AI parsing action
- [ ] Show effective allow/deny policy, field confidence, conflicts, and unresolved questions
- [ ] Add explicit confirmation and emergency-contact requirements
- [ ] Keep Level 1 or higher start action disabled until policy is valid and approved

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Blank scope visibly fails closed instead of implying no restrictions
- [ ] Users can correct every AI-interpreted field before approval
- [ ] Exclusions, limits, expiry, prohibited actions, and policy version are visible at confirmation
- [ ] Accessibility and error-state tests cover the complete preflight flow

title: Implement deterministic scope compiler and canonical policy hashing
milestone: v0.1.9 Assessment Authorization and Rules of Engagement
body:
Compile reviewed scope drafts into deterministic effective policies independently of the AI parser.

Tasks:
- [ ] Validate and normalize typed scope and RoE fields
- [ ] Apply precedence: platform deny, tenant deny, scope deny, scope allow, default deny
- [ ] Reject empty allow rules, invalid matchers, expired windows, absent limits, and policy conflicts
- [ ] Produce a human-readable effective-policy preview
- [ ] Canonicalize policy representation and generate a stable SHA-256 hash

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Semantically identical inputs produce the same canonical policy hash
- [ ] Compilation is deterministic and does not invoke an AI model
- [ ] Unsupported safety levels and unresolved broadening ambiguity fail closed
- [ ] Compiler decisions return stable machine-readable reason codes

title: Implement immutable scope versioning and approval binding
milestone: v0.1.9 Assessment Authorization and Rules of Engagement
body:
Bind validation authorization to an immutable AssessmentScopeVersion and exact canonical policy hash.

Tasks:
- [ ] Persist immutable scope versions and lifecycle states
- [ ] Bind approval to scope ID, version, policy hash, actor, safety level, and expiry
- [ ] Invalidate prior approval after any material scope or RoE change
- [ ] Support activation, supersession, expiration, revocation, and immediate scope shrink
- [ ] Add scope-extension request and reapproval workflow

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] A ValidationJob cannot start with a stale or mismatched approval
- [ ] Scope expansion always creates a new version and requires approval
- [ ] Scope shrink and revocation block queued actions immediately
- [ ] Version and approval transitions are append-oriented and auditable

title: Implement typed target matchers and deny precedence
milestone: v0.1.9 Assessment Authorization and Rules of Engagement
body:
Implement canonical target matching for exact hosts, domain suffixes, URL origins and paths, IP addresses, CIDR ranges, inventory assets, and cloud-resource identities.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define typed matcher schemas and normalization rules
- [ ] Make apex and subdomain wildcard semantics explicit
- [ ] Normalize IDNs, case, trailing dots, IP forms, ports, and URL origins safely
- [ ] Apply exclusions and higher-level denials before allow rules
- [ ] Add private, loopback, link-local, and cloud-metadata boundary controls

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Look-alike domains and suffix-boundary errors do not match
- [ ] Explicit exclusions override broad allow rules
- [ ] IPv4, IPv6, CIDR, IDN, URL, and cloud-identity tests cover edge cases
- [ ] Invalid or non-normalizable matcher input fails closed

title: Enforce scope at every target-facing runtime boundary
milestone: v0.1.9 Assessment Authorization and Rules of Engagement
body:
Integrate immutable scope evaluation into the Tool Gateway and scanner runtime so authorization is checked continuously rather than only at job creation.

Tasks:
- [ ] Require scope ID, version, policy hash, actor, tool, parameters, and normalized target in dispatch context
- [ ] Recheck after DNS resolution and immediately before connection
- [ ] Recheck redirects, discovered links and services, ports, protocols, and credential use
- [ ] Add long-running job checkpoints and queued-action reevaluation
- [ ] Deny on missing context, policy timeout, evaluator failure, or review_required

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] DNS rebinding and cross-origin redirect fixtures remain blocked
- [ ] Discovery produces evidence but never expands scope automatically
- [ ] Every target-facing ToolCall records the evaluated scope version and decision reason
- [ ] Policy-engine failure cannot fall back to execution

title: Implement Rules of Engagement limits and emergency stop
milestone: v0.1.9 Assessment Authorization and Rules of Engagement
body:
Enforce operational Rules of Engagement, including time windows, request rate, concurrency, crawl bounds, data-handling constraints, automated stop conditions, and manual emergency stop.

Tasks:
- [ ] Enforce timezone-aware execution windows
- [ ] Enforce request, concurrency, crawl-depth, and redirect limits
- [ ] Add target error-rate and contact-request stop conditions
- [ ] Add emergency stop that prevents dispatch and cancels queued work
- [ ] Define bounded cancellation and review-before-resume behavior

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Jobs cannot start or continue outside their approved window
- [ ] Rate and concurrency counters are safe across workers
- [ ] Emergency stop prevents new dispatches and records final state
- [ ] Resumption requires explicit review and a still-valid policy

title: Add authorization and scope audit events with stable reason codes
milestone: v0.1.9 Assessment Authorization and Rules of Engagement
body:
Make authorization, scope interpretation, approval, enforcement, and stop decisions explainable without leaking sensitive values.

Tasks:
- [ ] Define lifecycle audit events for authorization and scope versions
- [ ] Define allow, deny, and review-required reason-code taxonomy
- [ ] Record AI draft provenance and unresolved-question outcomes
- [ ] Record DNS, redirect, discovered-target, limit, and emergency-stop decisions
- [ ] Redact credentials, raw authorization documents, and sensitive target data

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Every blocked and allowed target-facing action has a stable reason code
- [ ] Audit records bind to scope version and policy hash
- [ ] Corrections create new events instead of rewriting history
- [ ] Redaction tests prevent protected values from entering general logs

title: Add adversarial assessment scope enforcement test suite
milestone: v0.1.9 Assessment Authorization and Rules of Engagement
body:
Create evaluation fixtures and end-to-end tests for the authorization, compilation, approval, and runtime enforcement contract.

Tasks:
- [ ] Cover blank, ambiguous, contradictory, expired, revoked, and cross-tenant scope
- [ ] Cover wildcard confusion, IDN look-alikes, IPv4/IPv6 normalization, and CIDR boundaries
- [ ] Cover DNS rebinding, private/link-local resolution, redirects, and discovered targets
- [ ] Cover stale approval, hash mismatch, policy timeout, rate limits, and emergency stop
- [ ] Add replayable expected decision reason codes

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] All fail-closed cases prove that no target-facing dispatch occurred
- [ ] Tests verify both policy result and audit evidence
- [ ] CI runs deterministic policy tests without external target access
- [ ] Regression fixtures are added for every discovered scope-escape defect
